params.name: Stele of Naram-Sin
params.description: The Stele of Naram-Sin is one of the earliest known commemorative relief steles. The relief on the stele depicts the victory scene of King Naram-Sin. Naram-Sin, the grandson of Sargon the Great, conquered many regions during his reign, greatly expanding the territory of the Akkadian Empire. The Stele of Naram-Sin is currently housed in the Louvre Museum and has become a valuable resource for studying the history and art of the ancient Near East.
